essay1:
The Nudge Fellowship is not just a free trip for me. I see it as a journey for learning, growth, and self-discovery. Traveling to Delhi and visiting some of the best universities in India is something I truly value, not because it is fully funded, but because it gives me a chance to learn beyond the classroom.

I strongly believe that real learning happens when we step out, meet new people, listen to different ideas, and see how great institutions work. Visiting campuses like IIT Delhi, JNU, JMI, AMU, and Delhi University will help me understand how students there think, study, and build their future. It will inspire me to aim higher in my own academic journey.

What I want to take back with me is more than just memories or a certificate. I want to return with new ideas, motivation, and a broader perspective about education and life. I hope this experience helps me become more confident, more focused, and more clear about my goals.

Most importantly, I want to bring back inspiration. Sometimes one journey can change the way we think about our future, and I believe this fellowship can be that turning point for me. I also want to share what I learn with my friends and classmates so that the benefit of this opportunity reaches others too.

For me, the Nudge Fellowship is not just a journey from Kerala to Delhi—it is a journey from a small dream to a clearer vision of my future. I come from a place where opportunities to experience institutions like Indian Institute of Technology Delhi feel distant. This fellowship brings that dream closer to reality.

I have always wished to study at IIT Delhi, but more than the destination, I want to understand the path—how students think, learn, and grow to reach there. Being in that environment, even briefly, will inspire me in ways that books alone cannot.

What I will take back is not just memories, but a stronger belief in myself. I will return with clarity about my goals, the confidence to pursue them, and the courage to aim higher than I ever thought possible. I will also carry back stories, ideas, and motivation to share with my friends, especially those who feel their dreams are too far away.

This fellowship will not end in Delhi—it will continue in every step I take afterward.

I’m drawn to the Nudge Fellowship because it brings together purpose and action in a way that feels real. I don’t just want to learn about social challenges from a distance—I want to work closely with communities, understand their lived experiences, and contribute in a meaningful way. The Fellowship’s focus on grassroots development, combined with structured learning and mentorship, feels like the right environment for me to grow both personally and professionally.

What stands out to me is the opportunity to step outside my comfort zone and engage deeply with issues like livelihoods, education, and access. I’m particularly interested in how small, consistent interventions can create long-term impact. Being in the field, working alongside community members and local organizations, will help me develop empathy, patience, and a more grounded perspective on problem-solving.

What I hope to take back with me is not just knowledge, but a shift in mindset. I want to become someone who listens better, adapts faster, and approaches challenges with humility. I also hope to build strong relationships—with peers, mentors, and community members—that will continue to shape my journey even after the Fellowship ends.

Ultimately, I see the Nudge Fellowship as a transformative experience—one that will challenge my assumptions, sharpen my sense of responsibility, and inspire me to stay committed to creating meaningful, lasting impact.

essay1:
The purpose of this letter is to express my formal intention to apply for the The/Nudge Fellowship programme. Currently, I am a student that is preparing for the CUET UG and am at a pivotal time where fulfilling my ambitions in academia needs to coincide with improving my skills holistically. My reason for this is that there is a huge disparity between what is being tested academically, through the traditional methods, compared to the skills you need to be ‘ready for life’ in today’s working world.


The/Nudge Fellowship provides the unique chance for me to fill that gap, given that (a) it has a very comprehensive curriculum; and (b) it has presence on six campuses in Delhi. I have two reasons for wanting to participate in the fellowship, to grow intellectually and to be empowered socially and financially. While my efforts spent preparing for the CUET will help me to develop a greater level of knowledge of the subject area I eventually will be studying in university; my participation in the The/Nudge Fellowship will also provide me with some of those crucial “nudges” necessary for developing my communication, critical thinking, and digital literacy skills.


I want to utilise this opportunity to continue to develop my professional etiquette and leadership style so, once I am successful in my attempt to secure my admission at university through the CUET, I will have the benefits of not only being an applicant with a qualification from university, but also being able to positively impact my career and contribute to India's workforce.


I anticipate being able to learn about myself through the process of being in this highly regarded programme and will give of my time back to the community that helped me become who I am today.

Growing up in Kasaragod, I have closely observed how communities interact with their environment. In my locality, improper waste disposal and the lack of segregation at the household level often resulted in waste accumulation in public spaces and nearby water channels. This created environmental and health concerns, highlighting a clear gap between awareness and everyday practices. These observations shaped my interest in understanding and addressing waste management challenges at the community level.
During my time in a residential campus, my interaction with society became largely limited to structured environments. While this reduced my direct engagement with community issues, it enhanced my ability to reflect critically on real-world problems and think about practical, long-term solutions.

name: sobiya salim m
essay1:
As a Bioscience student currently transitioning toward a career in design, I have always been fascinated by how structured systems can solve complex problems. I am drawn to The/Nudge Fellowship because it moves beyond traditional charity; it treats poverty alleviation as a challenge of engineering and scalability. My interest in interior design is rooted in the idea of "spatial wellness"—creating environments that improve human lives. However, I believe that before I can design spaces for people, I must first understand the social and economic ecosystems they live in. Joining The/Nudge is an opportunity for me to step out of the classroom and learn how a professional organization creates resilient livelihoods and systemic change. I want to be part of a community that values data-driven action and empathy, and I believe my background in science has given me the analytical mindset to contribute effectively to your mission.

From this fellowship, I expect to take back a "system-change" mindset that will define my future career. While I plan to pursue a B.Des and eventually a Master’s abroad, I do not want to be a designer who only works in a vacuum. I want to take back the ability to solve "wicked" problems using grit and practical teamwork. I expect to gain firsthand experience in navigating the complexities of ground-level implementation—skills that no textbook can provide. Most importantly, I will carry back a deeper sense of social responsibility. I want my future work in design to be informed by the realities of India’s development needs. This fellowship will provide me with the leadership foundation and the emotional intelligence required to ensure that my professional success also contributes to the progress of my country. I will leave with the confidence that I can turn big ideas into tangible, life-changing results.

name: Muhammed Swalih .k
essay1:
A conversation with a daily wage worker in my locality made me rethink what I understood about opportunity. Despite working long hours, his income remained uncertain, directly affecting his children’s education and daily stability. What stood out to me was not just the lack of resources, but how deeply livelihoods, education, and future prospects were connected. This experience pushed me to look beyond surface-level explanations and think more seriously about the structural nature of poverty.

As I continued my academic journey and interacted with people from different backgrounds, I began to see that many challenges are not isolated, but part of a larger system that requires thoughtful and sustained intervention. This realization strengthened my interest in understanding how livelihood-focused efforts can create more stable and lasting change.

The Nudge Fellowship appeals to me because of its strong emphasis on grassroots engagement and its commitment to improving livelihoods in a sustainable manner. I see this as an opportunity to learn directly from communities, understand their realities, and contribute meaningfully while developing a deeper perspective on social issues.

In the long term, I aspire to work in public service, potentially through the civil services. However, I believe that effective policymaking must be grounded in real-world experience. This Fellowship offers a valuable chance to build that foundation early in my journey. I bring with me a willingness to learn, adaptability, and a genuine intent to engage with and understand challenges at the grassroots level.

essay1:
I don’t see the Nudge Fellowship as just an opportunity to “do social work.” For me, it is a chance to question the way I already think. Growing up, most of what I know about inequality comes from books, news, or conversations, but all of that is still from a distance. I want to understand what lies behind those facts, the decisions people are forced to make, the trade-offs they live with, and the strengths they build in difficult situations. The fellowship feels like a space where I can unlearn my assumptions before trying to learn anything new.
What makes this important to me right now, as a 12th standard student, is that I am at a stage where my ideas about success, career, and purpose are still forming. I don’t want those ideas to be shaped only by marks or conventional paths. I want them to be shaped by real experiences. I believe this will help me make more thoughtful choices in the future, not just for myself but also in how I relate to others.
What I will take back is not just empathy, but a shift in how I observe the world. I will start paying attention to small details like how systems work, where they fail, and how people adapt to them. I will also take back the confidence to engage with unfamiliar situations without immediately judging or trying to control them.
Most importantly, I will carry a quieter but stronger sense of purpose that is the understanding that meaningful change often begins with listening, patience, and consistency than quick solutions.

However, due to my academic path, I have had limited opportunities to engage in fieldwork or social initiatives. essay1:
For a long time, I got used to staying quiet, even when I had something to say. That only started to change when I began debating and realised how much I enjoyed questioning things people usually accept without thinking twice.

Since then, I’ve started noticing how often we’re expected to accept things the way they are, especially in areas like education, law, and even basic opportunities around us. That’s a big reason why this fellowship stands out to me. It’s not just about visiting places like IIT Delhi or Delhi University, but about being in an environment where people actually question and challenge ideas openly.

I want to pay attention to how those conversations happen, how people disagree, how they defend their opinions, and how they think through issues that don’t have clear answers. That’s something I don’t get to see much around me.

When I come back, I don’t just want to keep that experience to myself. I want to bring that kind of thinking into my own space by conducting more structured debates and discussions in my school, focusing on real world issues instead of just competition topics. More than anything, I want to get better at thinking for myself and not just accepting things because “that’s how it is” but actually questioning who it works for and who it doesn’t.”

essay1: Because I want to know more about the future jobs and education, and I want to visit the major universities in Delhi

Visiting premium institutes in Delhi while preparing for entrance exams can really make a difference in a student’s journey. Delhi is known for having some of the best coaching centers in India, and experiencing them firsthand helps students understand what serious preparation actually looks like.

One of the biggest advantages is the chance to interact directly with experienced teachers. Instead of just studying on your own, you get to ask questions, clear doubts, and learn smarter ways to approach exams. This kind of guidance can boost confidence and make studying feel more manageable. These institutes also provide well-structured study materials and regular tests, which help students track their progress and stay on the right path.

Another important aspect is the environment. Being surrounded by hardworking and focused students creates a natural motivation to do better. It pushes you to stay consistent and disciplined, which is often the hardest part of exam preparation. You also get a clearer idea of where you stand compared to others, helping you improve in areas where you may be lacking.

Visiting these institutes also helps you decide if they are the right fit for you. You can observe the teaching style, facilities, and overall atmosphere before making a decision.

In the end, it’s not just about studying—it’s about gaining confidence, direction, and the right mindset to achieve your goals.

നഡ്ജ് ഫെല്ലോഷിപ്പ് (The Nudge Fellowship) കേവലം ഒരു സർട്ടിഫിക്കറ്റിനേക്കാൾ ഉപരിയായി, കരുത്തുറ്റ നേതൃപാടവത്തിലൂടെ സമൂഹത്തിലെ ഘടനാപരമായ വെല്ലുവിളികളെ മനസ്സിലാക്കാനുള്ള ഒരു വാതിലാണ്. "നഡ്ജ് തിയറി" (Nudge theory) അഥവാ ചെറിയ ഇടപെടലുകളിലൂടെ വലിയ സാമൂഹിക മാറ്റങ്ങൾ കൊണ്ടുവരാം എന്ന ആശയത്തിലുള്ള താൽപ്പര്യമാണ് എന്നെ ഈ ഫെല്ലോഷിപ്പിലേക്ക് ആകർഷിക്കുന്നത്..
ഈ ഫെല്ലോഷിപ്പിലൂടെ ലഭിക്കുന്ന അറിവും അനുഭവങ്ങളും എന്റെ ഭാവി പ്രവർത്തനങ്ങളിൽ വലിയ മാറ്റങ്ങൾ കൊണ്ടുവരുമെന്ന് ഞാൻ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്നു. പ്രത്യേകിച്ച്, പ്രാദേശികമായ പദ്ധതികളിൽ പ്രയോഗിക്കാൻ കഴിയുന്ന ഒരു മികച്ച പ്രവർത്തനശൈലി രൂപപ്പെടുത്താൻ എനിക്ക് സാധിക്കും. സാമൂഹിക ആവശ്യങ്ങളെ വ്യക്തമായ കർമ്മപദ്ധതികളായി മാറ്റാൻ കഴിയുന്ന ഒരു മികച്ച കമ്മ്യൂണിക്കേറ്ററായി മാറാനും ഇതിലൂടെ ഞാൻ ലക്ഷ്യമിടുന്നു

essay1:
I come from a small town in Kerala. I finished my MBA from Pondicherry University , qualified the JRF exam in management , and now I am trying to get into a PhD program. That sounds like a smooth journey when I write it like that, but honestly it has been anything but smooth.
Right now I am in a strange place. I have the qualification but I do not fully know the world I am trying to enter. I have read about IIT Delhi and JNU in textbooks and news articles, but I have never seen what real research culture actually looks like from the inside. Nobody around me has either. That gap is hard to explain to people who have not felt it.
When I saw the Nudge Fellowship I did not think about the certificate first. I thought about how different things could look after just one week of walking those campuses, talking to people who are living the academic life I am trying to build, and understanding what serious research communities actually feel like in person.
I am at the beginning of my PhD journey and the decisions I make now will shape everything that comes after. Getting this kind of exposure at exactly this point in my life is not just useful, it is important.
What I will bring back is not something I can put on a resume. It is the clarity of knowing what I am working toward and why. That kind of thing changes how you think, how you plan, and how far you are willing to go.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

ഇന്ത്യയുടെ തലസ്ഥാനമായ ഡൽഹിയിലെ ഐ.ഐ.ടി (IIT) ഉൾപ്പെടെയുള്ള പ്രമുഖ വിദ്യാഭ്യാസ സ്ഥാപനങ്ങൾ സന്ദർശിക്കാനുള്ള ഈ അവസരം എന്റെ കരിയറിലെ സുപ്രധാനമായ ഒരു നാഴികക്കല്ലായിരിക്കും. നിലവിൽ JEE പരീക്ഷയ്ക്കായി തയ്യാറെടുക്കുന്ന ഒരു വിദ്യാർത്ഥി എന്ന നിലയിൽ, രാജ്യത്തെ ഏറ്റവും മികച്ച ബുദ്ധികേന്ദ്രങ്ങൾ എങ്ങനെ പ്രവർത്തിക്കുന്നു എന്ന് നേരിട്ട് മനസ്സിലാക്കാൻ ഞാൻ ആഗ്രഹിക്കുന്നു. എഞ്ചിനീയറിംഗിൽ ഇലക്ട്രോണിക്സ് (Electronics) മേഖലയോടാണ് എനിക്ക് പ്രത്യേക താല്പര്യം. കേവലം പുസ്തകങ്ങളിൽ നിന്ന് പഠിക്കുന്നതിനപ്പുറം, ഈ സ്ഥാപനങ്ങളിലെ അത്യാധുനിക ലാബുകളും ഗവേഷണ രീതികളും നേരിട്ട് കാണുന്നത് എന്റെ ലക്ഷ്യങ്ങളെ കൂടുതൽ വ്യക്തമാക്കും.

കൂടാതെ, ഭാവിയിൽ സ്വന്തമായി ഒരു സ്റ്റാർട്ടപ്പ് (Startup) തുടങ്ങുക എന്നത് എന്റെ വലിയൊരു സ്വപ്നമാണ്. സാങ്കേതിക വിദ്യ എങ്ങനെയാണ് സാധാരണക്കാരുടെ പ്രശ്നങ്ങൾ പരിഹരിക്കാൻ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് എന്ന് മനസ്സിലാക്കാൻ 'The Nudge' പോലുള്ള പ്ലാറ്റ്‌ഫോമുകൾ സഹായിക്കും. ഈ സ്ഥാപനങ്ങളിലെ ഇന്നൊവേഷൻ സെന്ററുകളും സംരംഭകത്വ അന്തരീക്ഷവും നേരിട്ട് അനുഭവിക്കുന്നത് എന്റെ സ്വപ്നങ്ങളിലേക്ക് ഒരു വലിയ ചുവടുവെപ്പായിരിക്കും. രാജ്യത്തെ മികച്ച വിദഗ്ധരുമായും വിദ്യാർത്ഥികളുമായും സംവദിക്കുന്നത് എന്റെ ചിന്താഗതിയെ വിശാലമാക്കാൻ സഹായിക്കുമെന്ന് ഞാൻ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്നു.
ഈ സന്ദർശനത്തിന് ശേഷം വലിയൊരു ആത്മവിശ്വാസവും വ്യക്തമായ ഒരു ലക്ഷ്യബോധവുമായാകും ഞാൻ മടങ്ങുക. ഡൽഹിയിലെ പ്രമുഖ സ്ഥാപനങ്ങളിലെ പഠനരീതികളും അവിടുത്തെ വിദ്യാർത്ഥികളുടെ കഠിനാധ്വാനവും നേരിട്ട് കാണുന്നത് എന്റെ JEE തയ്യാറെടുപ്പുകൾക്ക് വലിയൊരു ഊർജ്ജം നൽകും. ഇലക്ട്രോണിക്സ് മേഖലയിലെ പുതിയ സാധ്യതകളെക്കുറിച്ചും ഒരു സ്റ്റാർട്ടപ്പ് എങ്ങനെ വിജയകരമായി കെട്ടിപ്പടുക്കാം എന്നതിനെക്കുറിച്ചും കൃത്യമായ ഒരു ധാരണ എനിക്ക് ലഭിക്കും. പ്രീമിയർ ഇൻസ്റ്റിറ്റ്യൂട്ടുകളിലെ രീതികൾ മനസ്സിലാക്കുന്നത് എന്റെ ആത്മവിശ്വാസം വർദ്ധിപ്പിക്കുകയും വലിയ മത്സര പരീക്ഷകൾക്ക് കൂടുതൽ കരുത്തോടെ തയ്യാറെടുക്കാൻ എന്നെ പ്രചോദിപ്പിക്കുകയും ചെയ്യും.

ഈ യാത്രയിൽ നിന്ന് ലഭിക്കുന്ന അറിവുകൾ എന്റെ സഹപാഠികളുമായി പങ്കുവെക്കാനും അവരെ വലിയ ലക്ഷ്യങ്ങളിലേക്ക് പ്രചോദിപ്പിക്കാനും ഞാൻ ശ്രമിക്കും. ഒരു മികച്ച എഞ്ചിനീയറായും സംരംഭകനായും മാറാൻ ആവശ്യമായ 'സ്കെയിൽ-ഫസ്റ്റ്' (Scale-first) ചിന്താഗതി ഈ യാത്രയിലൂടെ എനിക്ക് ലഭിക്കുമെന്ന് ഞാൻ ഉറച്ചു വിശ്വസിക്കുന്നു. ചുരുക്കത്തിൽ, രാജ്യത്തിന്റെ പുരോഗതിയിൽ എപ്രകാരം പങ്കാളിയാകാം എന്നതിനെക്കുറിച്ചുള്ള പുതിയൊരു കാഴ്ചപ്പാടാകും ഈ യാത്ര എനിക്ക് സമ്മാനിക്കുന്നത്.

essay1:
ഞാൻ കാസിം റഹീസ് എം, ഉത്തര മലബാറിന്റെ മണ്ണിൽ നിന്നാണ് വരുന്നത്. ചരിത്രവും സംസ്കാരവും ഏറെയുണ്ടെങ്കിലും, എന്റെ നാട് നേരിടുന്ന ചില അടിസ്ഥാന പ്രശ്നങ്ങൾ എന്നെ എപ്പോഴും ചിന്തിപ്പിക്കാറുണ്ട്. അവസരങ്ങളുടെ അഭാവം മൂലം നാടുവിടേണ്ടി വരുന്ന പ്രതിഭകളും, വിദ്യാഭ്യാസവും ആരോഗ്യവും എല്ലാവരിലേക്കും ഒരേപോലെ എത്താത്ത സാഹചര്യവും ഇന്നും അവിടെ നിലനിൽക്കുന്നു. ഈ പ്രശ്നങ്ങളെ വെറും കുറവുകളായല്ല, മറിച്ച് പരിഹരിക്കപ്പെടേണ്ട വെല്ലുവിളികളായാണ് ഞാൻ കാണുന്നത്. ഈ ബോധ്യമാണ് 'നഡ്ജ് ഫെല്ലോഷിപ്പ്' (The Nudge Fellowship) എന്നിലേക്ക് എന്നെ എത്തിക്കുന്നത്. ദാരിദ്ര്യം എന്നത് കേവലം പണമില്ലാത്ത അവസ്ഥയല്ല, മറിച്ച് അന്തസ്സോടെ ജീവിക്കാനുള്ള മനുഷ്യന്റെ സാധ്യതകൾ തടയപ്പെടുന്ന നിസ്സഹായാവസ്ഥയാണ്. എന്റെ നാടിന്റെയും സമൂഹത്തിന്റെയും ഈ അവസ്ഥയ്ക്ക് ഒരു മാറ്റം കൊണ്ടുവരാൻ കേവലം വാക്കുകളല്ല, പ്രായോഗികമായ പ്രവർത്തികളാണ് ആവശ്യമെന്ന് ഞാൻ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്നു.

മാറ്റം എന്നത് പുറമെ നിന്ന് അടിച്ചേൽപ്പിക്കേണ്ട ഒന്നല്ല, മറിച്ച് ഓരോ വ്യക്തിയുടെയും ഉള്ളിലെ നഷ്ടപ്പെട്ടുപോയ ആത്മവിശ്വാസത്തെ വീണ്ടെടുക്കലാണ്. 'നഡ്ജ്' (Nudge) എന്നത് എനിക്ക് ഒരു പരിശീലനമല്ല, മറിച്ച് വിഘടിച്ചുനിൽക്കുന്ന സാമൂഹിക വ്യവസ്ഥകളെ സ്നേഹത്തിന്റെയും സഹാനുഭൂതിയുടെയും നൂലുകൊണ്ട് തുന്നിച്ചേർക്കാനുള്ള ഒരു നിയോഗമാണ്. സിദ്ധാന്തങ്ങൾക്കപ്പുറം, മനുഷ്യന്റെ വേദനകളിലേക്ക് ഇറങ്ങിച്ചെന്ന് അവയെ പ്രായോഗിക പരിഹാരങ്ങളായി പരിവർത്തനം ചെയ്യുന്ന ഒരു ചാലകശക്തിയാകാൻ ഞാൻ ആഗ്രഹിക്കുന്നു. യഥാർത്ഥ വികസനം എന്നത് ഒരാളെ സഹായിക്കുന്നതല്ല, മറിച്ച് ആ സഹായം ഇനി ഒരിക്കലും ആവശ്യമില്ലാത്ത വിധം അവരെ സ്വയം പ്രാപ്തരാക്കുന്നതാണ്.

ഈ ഫെല്ലോഷിപ്പ് പൂർത്തിയാക്കി മടങ്ങുമ്പോൾ, എന്റെ കൂടെ ഉണ്ടാവുക വലിയൊരു ദർശനമായിരിക്കും (Vision). സങ്കീർണ്ണമായ വെല്ലുവിളികളെ ശാന്തതയോടെ നേരിടാനുള്ള ആന്തരിക കരുത്തും, അദൃശ്യമായ മനുഷ്യബന്ധങ്ങളെ ശാക്തീകരിക്കാനുള്ള നേതൃത്വപാടവവും ഞാൻ എന്നിൽ നിക്ഷേപിക്കും. ലോകത്തെ ഭരിക്കാനല്ല, മറിച്ച് ലോകത്തെ സേവിക്കാനുള്ള ഏറ്റവും വലിയ അധികാരം 'സ്നേഹം കലർന്ന പ്രവൃത്തി' ആണെന്ന് ഞാൻ ഇതിലൂടെ തെളിയിക്കും. തിരികെ പോകുമ്പോൾ എന്റെ സ്വത്വം പഴയതിനേക്കാൾ കൂടുതൽ വിശാലമായിരിക്കും—അവിടെ 'ഞാൻ' എന്നതിനേക്കാൾ 'നമ്മൾ' എന്ന ബോധമായിരിക്കും പ്രകാശിക്കുക. ലോകത്തെ മാറ്റിമറിക്കാൻ ഒരു മഹാവിസ്ഫോടനം ആവശ്യമില്ല, മറിച്ച് ശരിയായ ദിശയിലുള്ള ഒരു ചെറിയ 'നഡ്ജ്' മതിയാകുമെന്ന വലിയ സത്യമായിരിക്കും ഞാൻ കൊണ്ടുപോകുന്ന ഏറ്റവും വലിയ സമ്പാദ്യം. കാസിം റഹീസ് എന്ന വ്യക്തിയിൽ നിന്നും സമൂഹത്തിന് മാറ്റം നൽകുന്ന ഒരു വലിയ ശക്തിയായി ഞാൻ എന്നെത്തന്നെ രൂപപ്പെടുത്തും.

The Nudge Fellowship attracts me because it represents a space where learning goes beyond textbooks and connects directly with real-world challenges. As a Humanities student, I have developed a strong curiosity about society, why inequalities exist, how systems function, and how small interventions can create meaningful change. I often find myself exploring topics beyond my syllabus, trying to connect ideas from different disciplines to better understand the world around me.

At the same time, I am deeply aware that understanding social issues is not enough without engaging with them directly. The Fellowship offers an opportunity to step into real communities, listen to people’s experiences, and learn from ground realities. This is something I actively seek to move from observation to meaningful engagement.

I also see this as a chance to grow personally. I am someone who reflects on my strengths and areas of improvement, and I take initiative to learn and improve myself. Being part of this program will challenge me to step out of my comfort zone, develop practical problem-solving skills, and build confidence in unfamiliar situations.

What I will take back is not just knowledge, but a more empathetic and grounded perspective. I hope to develop leadership qualities rooted in understanding and collaboration, where I can positively influence others and contribute to a shared goal. In the long run, I want to use these experiences to shape my academic journey and future contributions to society in a meaningful way.

All these are the reasons why I chose the Nudge Fellowship.

The Nudge Fellowship stands out to me because it focuses on working directly with communities and creating real, ground-level impact. I’m drawn to the idea of not just learning about social problems, but actually living within communities, understanding their challenges, and working alongside them to find solutions. The Fellowship’s emphasis on leadership, empathy, and problem-solving matches the kind of person I want to become.
Coming from Kerala, I’ve seen both development and gaps—especially in areas like education, employment, and access to opportunities for youth. I want to understand these issues more deeply, beyond surface-level assumptions. The Nudge Fellowship gives a platform to do that in a structured, meaningful way while being guided by mentors and a strong peer network.
What I will take back is not just knowledge, but perspective. I want to bring back a stronger sense of empathy, the ability to listen better, and practical skills in solving real-world problems. I also hope to build confidence in taking initiative and leading change, even in small ways.
Most importantly, I want to take back a mindset—that change is possible when we stay consistent and work closely with people. Whether I continue in the social sector or move into another field, this experience will shape how I think, act, and contribute to society.

At the same time, I don’t want to keep that experience to myself. I would like to share what I learn with people around me—especially friends and juniors who may not get similar opportunities—so that it creates a wider impact.
In the end, I see this fellowship as a starting point for growth, not just for myself, but for the community I’m part of.

name: muhammad hafeez
essay1: The/Nudge Indian Administrative Fellowship 2026: An Academic OverviewThe Indian Administrative Fellowship (IAF), orchestrated by The/Nudge Institute, is a highly selective, 18-month program designed to foster rigorous public-private synergy. It strategically embeds senior private-sector leaders within the Indian government, allowing them to collaborate directly with top-tier civil servants, such as Principal Secretaries and Additional Chief Secretaries. The 2026 cohort will continue to focus on conceptualizing and executing population-scale, technology-driven interventions to enhance public service delivery and cultivate resilient livelihoods.  For an ambitious professional like Muhammad Hafeez, the IAF represents a transformative platform to apply intellectual rigor to public administration. The fellowship specifically seeks individuals who possess both strategic acumen and a profound intent to drive systemic socioeconomic change.  Core Components of the Fellowship:Strategic Implementation: Fellows are tasked with solving complex governance challenges by applying private-sector efficiency and technological innovation to public-sector frameworks.  Capacity Building: The program includes rigorous leadership development modules facilitated by premier policy institutions such as NITI Aayog.  Scale and Impact: Participants design developmental frameworks that mobilize significant public financing, directly impacting millions of citizens across sectors like agriculture, education, and rural development.  Pursuing the IAF aligns with a resolute commitment to sustainable governance. For candidates driven by deep ambition, the fellowship offers an unparalleled opportunity to architect data-driven policies and actively engineer the vision of a poverty-free India.

essay1:
ചാലിയാർ പുഴയുടെ തീരത്തെ പ്രഭാതങ്ങളും കടലിൽ അധ്വാനിക്കുന്ന മത്സ്യത്തൊഴിലാളികളുടെ ജീവിതവുമാണ് എന്നിലെ സാമൂഹിക ബോധത്തെ രൂപപ്പെടുത്തിയത്. മത്സ്യത്തൊഴിലാളിയായ റിയാസിനെ പോലുള്ളവരുമായി സംസാരിച്ചപ്പോൾ, ജീവൻ പണയം വെച്ചുള്ള അവരുടെ സാഹസങ്ങളാണ് നമ്മുടെ നാടിന്റെ പട്ടിണിയകറ്റുന്നതെന്ന് ഞാൻ തിരിച്ചറിഞ്ഞു. ഈ കഠിനാധ്വാനവും പോരാട്ടവീര്യവുമാണ്, പരിമിതമായ സാഹചര്യങ്ങളിൽ നിന്നും വിജ്ഞാനത്തിന്റെ ലോകത്തേക്ക് വരാൻ എനിക്ക് പ്രചോദനമായത്. നിലവിൽ  മത ഭൗതിക സമന്വയ വിദ്യാഭ്യാസം നൽകുന്ന ഒരു റസിഡൻഷ്യൽ ക്യാമ്പസിലാണ് ഞാൻ പഠിക്കുന്നത്.

ക്യാമ്പസ് ജീവിതത്തിൽ ഞാൻ നേരിടുന്ന വലിയൊരു വെല്ലുവിളിയാണ് ഗൗരവകരമായ കാര്യങ്ങളെ നിസ്സാരവൽക്കരിക്കുന്ന ഒരു 'vibe-only' സംസ്കാരം. ‘തന്ത വൈബ്’ എന്ന് വിളിച്ച് പരിഹസിക്കുന്ന ഈ പ്രവണത ഇന്ന് വ്യാപകമാണ് (‘പഴഞ്ചൻ’ അല്ലെങ്കിൽ ‘ഔട്ട്‌ഡേറ്റഡ്’ ആയ ചിന്താഗതിക്കാരൻ എന്നാണ് ഇതുകൊണ്ട് അവർ അർത്ഥമാക്കുന്നത്). അറിവിനെയും രാഷ്ട്രീയ ബോധത്തെയും 'തന്ത വൈബ്' എന്ന് വിളിച്ച് അപരവൽക്കരിക്കുമ്പോൾ  യുവത്വം അർത്ഥവത്തായ ചർച്ചകളിൽ നിന്ന് അകന്നുപോകുന്നു. ക്യാമ്പസിലെ ലാംഗ്വേജ് ക്ലബ്ബിന്റെ കൺവീനറായി ഒരു പരിപാടി പ്ലാൻ ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ, “ഇതൊന്നും ഒരു വൈബില്ലാത്ത പരിപാടിയാണെ”ന്ന് പറഞ്ഞ് പലരും എന്നെ പരിഹസിച്ചിട്ടുണ്ട്. ഈ ഒരു ദുഷ്പ്രവണത ഭാവിയിൽ വലിയൊരു നേതൃത്വ വിടവ് ഉണ്ടാക്കുമെന്ന് ഞാൻ ഭയക്കുന്നു. ഇത്തരം വെല്ലുവിളികളെ വിവേകത്തോടെ എങ്ങനെ തരണം ചെയ്യാമെന്നതിനെ ക്കുറിച്ചുള്ള ആലോചനകളും അനുഭവങ്ങളും ഈ ഫെലോഷിപ്പിലൂടെ ലഭിക്കുമെന്ന്നാണ് ഞാൻ പ്രതീക്ഷിക്കുന്നത്.

​IIT ഡൽഹി, JNU, AMU, JMI, DU തുടങ്ങിയ പ്രമുഖ സർവ്വകലാശാലകളിലെ സന്ദർശനത്തിലൂടെയും, വൈവിധ്യമാർന്ന സാമൂഹിക ചുറ്റുപാടുകൾ മനസ്സിലാക്കുന്നതിലൂടെയും എന്റെ കാഴ്ചപ്പാടുകൾ വിപുലമാക്കാൻ സാധിക്കുമെന്നാണ്. 
പ്രഗത്ഭരായ പ്രൊഫസർമാരുടെയും വിദഗ്ധരുടെയും സെഷനുകൾ എന്റെ ചിന്തകളെ കൂടുതൽ വിശാലമാക്കാൻ സഹായിക്കുമെന്നാണ് പ്രതീക്ഷ. ഡൽഹിയുടെ ചരിത്രവും പാരമ്പര്യവും നേരിട്ട് അനുഭവിക്കുന്നതിനൊപ്പം മികച്ച അഞ്ച് സമപ്രായക്കാരുമായുള്ള കൂട്ടായ്മ എനിക്ക് പുതിയ അറിവുകൾ നൽകും. ഈ ഫെലോഷിപ്പിലെ വർക്ക്ഷോപ്പുകൾ എന്നിലെ കഴിവുകളെ സ്വയം തിരിച്ചറിയാനും മികച്ച ലീഡർഷിപ്പ് ക്വാളിറ്റി വികസിപ്പിക്കാനും എന്നെ സഹായിക്കും.

ഈ ഫെലോഷിപ്പിലൂടെ ഡൽഹി യൂണിവേഴ്സിറ്റിയിൽ ബി.എ പൊളിറ്റിക്കൽ സയൻസിന് ചേരാൻ ലക്ഷ്യമിടുന്ന എനിക്ക് ഒരു വലിയ കരുത്താവും.
​ഈ ഫെലോഷിപ്പിലൂടെ ലഭിക്കുന്ന അറിവും സർട്ടിഫിക്കറ്റും ഉപയോഗിച്ച് എന്റെ ക്യാമ്പസിലെ കൗമാരക്കാർക്കിടയിൽ ഗുണപരമായ മാറ്റം കൊണ്ടുവരാൻ ഞാൻ പരിശ്രമിക്കും. ഗൗരവമുള്ള ചർച്ചകൾ അത്യന്താപേക്ഷിതമാണെന്ന് അവരെ ബോധ്യപ്പെടുത്താനും എല്ലാവരെയും ഉൾക്കൊള്ളുന്ന ഒരു നല്ല വ്യക്തിത്വമായി വളരാനും ഇത് എന്നെ സഹായിക്കും. ഐ.എ.എസ് (IAS) എന്ന എന്റെ ലക്ഷ്യത്തിലേക്കുള്ള യാത്രയിൽ ഈ ഫെലോഷിപ്പ് വലിയൊരു കരുത്താകുമെന്ന് ഞാൻ പ്രതീക്ഷിക്കുന്നു.

name: MUHAMMED YAMIN THASLIM
essay1:
I am currently preparing for CUET, and like many students, my world has been limited to textbooks, mock tests, and marks. While this phase is important, it has also made me realize that I don’t want my learning to be confined to an exam. I want to understand what lies beyond it how ideas are discussed, challenged, and applied in real academic spaces.
The Nudge Fellowship stands out to me because it offers exactly that exposure. Visiting institutions like Indian Institute of Technology Delhi and Jawaharlal Nehru University is not just about seeing campuses, but about experiencing environments where curiosity drives learning. I want to observe how students think, what questions they ask, and how they approach their goals.
What I hope to take back is clarity. Clarity about what I truly want to study, the kind of environment I want to grow in, and the effort required to reach there. I also want to bring back a broader perspective one that goes beyond my current routine and helps me make more informed decisions about my future.
More than anything, I believe this experience will shift my mindset from simply preparing for an exam to actively preparing for a meaningful academic journey.

name: Fathima  Hisha pv
essay1: I know it will be very useful to students.Also this is my dream to visit the premium institute.so nudge fellowship conducted this wonderful opportunity for students

I come from a hilly, rural village in Kottayam, Kerala. As a young woman from a marginalized community, I have always seen education as my primary way to build a life of purpose. My resilience was tested early: during my 10th-grade boards, I lost my mother and suddenly became the primary caregiver for my five-year-old brother. Balancing grief with household responsibilities was difficult, yet I secured an "85%". This taught me that I am capable of managing immense pressure while staying focused on my goals.

My vision is to become a  Sports Psychologist. I am fascinated by how the mind influences performance, but I know this is an unconventional path in India. My plan is to eventually study abroad, but first, I want to build a rock-solid foundation at a premier institution like "Ashoka" or "Azim Premji University", where learning is holistic.

The Nudge Fellowship is the bridge I need to reach this goal. While I want to study the mind, I also need to understand the systems that support it. The "National Capital Region"  is the heart of India’s sports federations and policy-making. Being there will allow me to see how national-level institutions function and how mental health is prioritized (or neglected) at the highest levels. 

I want to move beyond textbooks to understand society’s "big picture." This exposure will help me see how sports psychology fits into the larger framework of Indian society. I seek guidance from mentors to clarify my path and build the self-reliance needed for a global career. When I return home to Kottayam, I want to carry a "sack full of experiences", new skills and the confidence to pursue a dream that few in my village have dared to dream.

What excites me most is the chance to learn from experts and be part of an environment that encourages curiosity, discipline, and critical thinking. I believe this experience will help me develop not only technical knowledge but also important life skills such as communication, teamwork, and problem-solving. From the Nudge Fellowship, I hope to take back a deeper understanding of my field of interest, clarity about my future goals, and the confidence to explore new opportunities. I also want to carry forward the mindset of continuous learning and the ability to connect academic concepts with real-life applications. Overall, I believe this Fellowship will play an important role in shaping my academic and personal growth.

essay1:
Visiting premium institutes in Delhi while preparing for entrance exams can really make a difference in a student’s journey. Delhi is known for having some of the best coaching centers in India, and experiencing them firsthand helps students understand what serious preparation actually looks like.

One of the biggest advantages is the chance to interact directly with experienced teachers. Instead of just studying on your own, you get to ask questions, clear doubts, and learn smarter ways to approach exams. This kind of guidance can boost confidence and make studying feel more manageable. These institutes also provide well-structured study materials and regular tests, which help students track their progress and stay on the right path.

Another important aspect is the environment. Being surrounded by hardworking and focused students creates a natural motivation to do better. It pushes you to stay consistent and disciplined, which is often the hardest part of exam preparation. You also get a clearer idea of where you stand compared to others, helping you improve in areas where you may be lacking.

Visiting these institutes also helps you decide if they are the right fit for you. You can observe the teaching style, facilities, and overall atmosphere before making a decision.

In the end, it’s not just about studying—it’s about gaining confidence, direction, and the right mindset to achieve your goals.

While this environment has shaped my discipline and values, it has also limited my opportunities to interact beyond my campus, as most of my time is spent within its boundaries.

In my surroundings, I have observed that farmers, despite their hard work, struggle due to unstable income and rising cultivation costs. At the same time, many people living in poverty find it difficult to meet even basic needs such as food and shelter. These observations have made me reflect more deeply on inequality and question the conditions that allow such disparities to continue.

name: HAMNA  PH
essay1:
I am not applying for a free trip to Delhi. I am applying because I need to see the battlefield before the war begins.
I am preparing for JEE Main  Indian Navy B.Tech Entry Scheme — two of the most competitive gateways in the country. As a kickboxing champion, I understand one truth deeply: you cannot win a fight you have never visualised. Champions study their opponents. I need to study my arena.
IIT Delhi, JNU, Delhi University — these are not just campuses. They are ecosystems of excellence that shape the engineers and thinkers who build this nation. Walking those corridors, breathing that atmosphere, speaking to students who are living the life I am training for — that is intelligence no textbook can give me.
The Nudge Fellowship is the only program that offers a +2 student like me a fully immersive, expert-guided exposure to premier institutions — at zero cost, zero compromise. For a first-generation dreamer fighting for a Navy engineering seat, this is not an opportunity. This is a strategic necessity.

I will take back three things that no coaching centre sells:
1. Clarity of Vision
Right now, IIT Delhi and the Navy B.Tech path exist in my mind as concepts. After walking those campuses, they will exist as memories — real, vivid, emotionally charged. A kickboxer who has seen the ring fights differently than one who has only imagined it. I will return to Kerala with a concrete mental image of exactly where I am going. That image will fuel every early morning study session.
2. A Benchmark for Excellence
Meeting students and experts from India's top institutions will show me the real standard — not the inflated version, not the discouraging version, but the honest, human version. I will understand what separates a qualifier from a topper, and I will calibrate my preparation accordingly.
3. Proof That I Belong
I am a kickboxing champion. I have stood in front of opponents bigger, older, and more experienced — and I have won by refusing to shrink. The Nudge Fellowship will be my first step onto a national stage. Returning home with a Fellowship Certificate will not just be an achievement on paper — it will be evidence, to myself and my community, that a student from Kerala can compete, contribute, and lead at any level in this country.

"A champion does not wait to be inspired. A champion goes and finds the inspiration, then brings it home."

That is exactly what I will do.

Statement of Interest: The/Nudge Indian Administrative Fellowship 2026The/Nudge Indian Administrative Fellowship (IAF) 2026 stands as a premier 18-month initiative that synergizes corporate strategic excellence with the scale of public administration. Established to accelerate systemic transformation, the fellowship embeds experienced professionals alongside senior civil servants—often at the Principal Secretary level—to tackle complex, population-scale challenges. By prioritizing technological integration, policy innovation, and resilient livelihood generation, the IAF enables its cohorts to directly impact millions of lives across participating state governments.  My name is Muhammad Rufaid, and it is my profound ambition to contribute to this transformative mandate as a 2026 Fellow. I am deeply drawn to the fellowship’s core philosophy of leveraging cross-sector collaboration to drive sustainable, socio-economic advancement. My strong interest stems from a resolute commitment to public service and a desire to apply my specialized competencies toward alleviating structural inequities. Securing a position within the IAF would present an unparalleled opportunity to engage in high-impact governance, navigating the complexities of large-scale program implementation while designing resilient public systems. I am highly motivated to bring an entrepreneurial, data-driven methodology to state-level administrative frameworks, ultimately playing a substantive role in The/Nudge Institute’s vision of realizing a poverty-free and technologically empowered India.
name: muhammed rufaid
ziyad abdulla
essay1:
In my village, there was a boy who had stopped trying. He failed in school, sat alone, and everyone called him lazy. One day, an old teacher told him quietly: "Just try one small thing today."

That one sentence changed him. He started small. Then bigger. Today, he runs a small shop and helps his family.

That moment taught me a simple truth: Sometimes people don't need big help. They just need one small nudge.

That is why I want the Nudge Fellowship. It believes in this idea:
"Don't push people. Just remind them of what they can do."

I also believe:

Dignity comes first. Never make anyone feel small while helping them.

Small actions matter. A kind word, a timely reminder, a simple plan – these change lives.

We change together. When a whole community gives small nudges, systems begin to move.

What will I take back?

Three simple things:

The skill to design small, smart triggers – not force, but gentle cues.

The habit of using data kindly – to understand people, not judge them.

A network of fellows – to learn and share what works.

When I return home, I will start a small "Nudge Corner" under that old tree. There, anyone who feels stuck can come. Not for lectures. Just for one small, honest reminder:
"You still can."

"Big change does not need big noise. Sometimes it just needs a quiet nudge."

I want to join The/Nudge Fellowship because I’ve reached a stage where I want to apply my expertise in educational Planning/Strategic Management to India’s most pressing developmental challenges.
I think the Nudge provides the exact platform needed to operate at the intersection of innovation and administration.

Unlike most students, for me, science was never confined only within the classroom walls. The seeds for such a philosophy were sown at the hands of my sister, Nida. She used to substitute all traditional fairy tales at bedtimes with her bedtime stories – stories of scientific breakthroughs. Rather than giving me new knowledge, those bedtime stories helped spark a thirst within me to know more about unseen phenomena that rule our universe, starting from the gravitational forces of the Great Attractor and ending with the quantum abilities of superconductors.

The love of "building things from scratch" gave birth to one of my projects – a tool called CarbonLint. By creating CarbonLint, I have learned that working on global problems requires not only good software engineering skills but a deep understanding of physics as well. While preparing for competitive entrance exams like JEE Main, NEET, and CUET in the meantime, trying to enter undergraduate universities in Japan, my main goal is to treat such exams not as a way to pass another test, but as acquiring knowledge necessary for a career in Material Physics.

My application to the Nudge Fellowship program is motivated by the desire to be part of a community of people who see technology as a catalyst of social development. My expectations from the Nudge Fellowship program are learning a better approach towards scientific leadership and getting guidance on how to take my projects out of private repositories and into real life. The Nudge Fellowship isn’t simply travel. It’s a chance to learn things books never teach. From Kerala, my life revolved around grades. Yet growth sneaks in when comfort fades. essay1:
Statement of Purpose – Application for The/Nudge Fellowship

I am currently a student at Darul Huda Islamic University in Tirur, Kerala. While my academic journey has been structured and supported, my understanding of society has grown through closely observing the lives of people around me.

I come from a financially stable background, where I have had the opportunity to focus on my education. However, many of my close friends come from middle-class families where financial pressure is a daily reality. I have seen them go to work sites regularly to support their families, even while continuing their studies. There were times when I went to meet them, but they had already left for work. In some situations, I also had to be absent from my classes.

These experiences made me question why capable students are forced to balance education and survival at such a young age. It also helped me understand that the issue is not only poverty, but also a lack of awareness, guidance, and access to the right opportunities.

While exploring my own interests, I started learning digital skills such as UI/UX design through online platforms. This showed me that learning today is not limited to classrooms. I began sharing these resources with a few of my friends—guiding them towards free courses and encouraging them to continue learning alongside their work. Even these small efforts helped me realize how powerful the right guidance can be.

The problem I want to work on is the situation where students are forced to start working early, often at the cost of their education and long-term growth. I believe this can be addressed by using digital technologies to create awareness about flexible learning options and skill-based education.

If selected for The/Nudge Fellowship by The/Nudge Institute, I would focus on helping students access free and flexible online learning resources, while also encouraging peer learning where they can support each other. My goal is to help students balance earning and learning, and gradually move towards better opportunities.

I understand that I am still at the beginning of my journey. However, I bring with me real-life observations, a questioning mindset, and a strong willingness to learn from communities. I am ready to step out of my comfort zone and contribute sincerely at the grassroots level.

For me, this fellowship is not just an opportunity—it is a responsibility to act on what I have seen and experienced and to be part of meaningful change.

Thank you for considering my application.

Sincerely,

Nudge Fellowship എന്നത് എനിക്ക് ക്ലാസ്‌റൂമിന് പുറത്തേക്കു പോകാനും ഇന്ത്യയിലെ മികച്ച കോളേജുകളിലെ പഠന അന്തരീക്ഷം നേരിട്ട് അനുഭവിക്കാനും കിട്ടുന്ന ഒരു വലിയ അവസരമാണ് എന്ന് ഞാൻ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്നു. കേരളത്തിൽ നിന്നുള്ള ഒരു പ്ലസ് വൺ വിദ്യാർത്ഥിയായ എന്നെ സംബന്ധിച്ച്, പുതിയ കാര്യങ്ങൾ പഠിക്കാനും, വ്യത്യസ്തമായ ചിന്താഗതികൾ അറിയാനും, വിദഗ്ധരുമായി സംസാരിക്കാനും വലിയ ആഗ്രഹമുണ്ട്. പഠനം യഥാർത്ഥ ജീവിതത്തിൽ എങ്ങനെ പ്രയോജനപ്പെടുന്നു എന്നത് മനസ്സിലാക്കാനും ഈ അവസരം എനിക്ക് സഹായിക്കും. ഇത് എന്റെ കാഴ്ചപ്പാട് വിപുലീകരിക്കുകയും എന്റെ പഠനത്തിലും വ്യക്തിജീവിതത്തിലും കൂടുതൽ ഉയർന്ന ലക്ഷ്യങ്ങൾ ലക്ഷ്യമിടാൻ പ്രചോദനമാകുകയും ചെയ്യും.
പ്രത്യേകിച്ച് മികച്ച ക്യാമ്പസുകൾ സന്ദർശിച്ച് മെന്റർമാരിൽ നിന്ന് നേരിട്ട് പഠിക്കാനുള്ള അവസരമാണ് എന്നെ ഏറ്റവും ആകർഷിക്കുന്നത്. ഇത് എന്റെ ഭാവിയെക്കുറിച്ചുള്ള കൂടുതൽ വ്യക്തത നൽകും, പ്രത്യേകിച്ച് സയൻസ് മേഖലയിലും ഉയർന്ന പഠനത്തിലും. കഴിവുള്ള വിദ്യാർത്ഥികളോടൊപ്പം സമയം ചെലവഴിക്കുന്നത് എന്നെ കൂടുതൽ മെച്ചപ്പെടാൻ പ്രചോദിപ്പിക്കുകയും നല്ല ബന്ധങ്ങൾ നിർമ്മിക്കാൻ സഹായിക്കുകയും ചെയ്യും.
ഈ ഫെല്ലോഷിപ്പിൽ നിന്ന് ഞാൻ കൊണ്ടുപോകുന്നത് അറിവ് മാത്രം അല്ല, ഒരു പുതിയ മനോഭാവവും ആണ്. ആത്മവിശ്വാസം വർധിപ്പിക്കാനും, ആശയവിനിമയ കഴിവുകൾ മെച്ചപ്പെടുത്താനും, എന്റെ ശക്തികളും മെച്ചപ്പെടുത്തേണ്ട മേഖലകളും കൂടുതൽ വ്യക്തമായി മനസ്സിലാക്കാനും ഇത് സഹായിക്കും. കൂടാതെ, ഞാൻ നേടുന്ന അനുഭവങ്ങൾ എന്റെ സുഹൃത്തുക്കളോടും ക്ലാസ്‌മേറ്റ്സിനോടും പങ്കുവെച്ച് അവരെയും ഇത്തരം അവസരങ്ങൾ ലക്ഷ്യമിടാൻ പ്രേരിപ്പിക്കാനാണ് എന്റെ ആഗ്രഹം.
ഒടുവില്‍, Nudge Fellowship എന്നത് എന്റെ ലക്ഷ്യങ്ങൾ രൂപപ്പെടുത്താനും എന്റെ ഭാവിയിലേക്കുള്ള ഒരു ശക്തമായ തുടക്കം നൽകാനും സഹായിക്കുന്ന ഒരു മാറ്റം വരുത്തുന്ന അനുഭവമായിരിക്കും.

name: Muhammed Faheem
essay1:
The/Nudge Fellowship (Indian Administrative Fellowship) in Delhi, leveraging my background in history and social structures to support the creation of a resilient, poverty-free India. I aim to contribute to the "academic ecosystem" by bridging the gap between historical policy analysis and modern administrative execution.
Statement of Purpose: India’s development challenges require a deep understanding of the socio-economic fabric that has shaped our current landscape. As a student of History, I bring a unique perspective to the fellowship’s mission. Understanding the evolution of economic thought and the Salient Features of the Indian Constitution allows me to approach policy implementation with a view of long-term sustainability rather than short-term fixes.
Proposed Contribution: I intend to focus on the Economic Inclusion Program and the Social Defence initiatives within the Delhi administrative framework. My goal is to:
• Analyze Policy Gaps: Use historical data and constitutional norms to identify why certain welfare schemes face friction in specific linguistic or regional demographics.
• Strengthen Ecosystem Collaboration: Facilitate smoother communication between government bodies and civil society by drafting data-driven reports that translate complex administrative goals into actionable community insights.
• Digital Integration: Support the transition toward digital public infrastructure, ensuring that technology serves as an equalizer for marginalized communities rather than a barrier.
Why The/Nudge? The/Nudge Institute provides a "most powerful academic ecosystem" because it removes the financial barrier to high-level social impact. By offering this platform for free, it attracts the most dedicated talent. I am eager to utilize the mentorship from senior IAS officers and the curriculum from institutional partners (like ISB) to refine my grit and humility as a change-maker.
Expected Outcome: Through this 18-month journey, I aim to deliver a scalable project charter that enhances the visibility of scholarships and educational empowerment initiatives, ensuring that the legacy of India’s social welfare reaches the last mile.

You should choose me for this fellowship because I am genuinely willing to learn and make the most of this opportunity. As a high school student, I am still figuring things out, but I am serious about my future and ready to grow. I plan to pursue my undergraduate studies at a good university and build a strong foundation, with the goal of becoming an IAS officer. I know this path requires understanding society, people, and real world issues, and I see this fellowship as an important first step. What I will take back is not just knowledge, but a broader perspective and better thinking skills. I will use what I learn in my studies and future career, and I will also share it with others around me so that the impact goes beyond just me.

name: Muhammed Sulthan K Sulthan
essay1:
I see the Nudge Fellowship as a place where questions meet action. As a Class 11 student from Kerala, I’ve grown up seeing both talent and untapped potential around me. Many of my friends are smart, curious, and hardworking, but lack exposure to real-world problem solving and mentors who push them to think bigger. That gap is what draws me to Nudge.

I don’t just want to study concepts from textbooks — I want to understand how ideas create impact on the ground. Nudge’s focus on entrepreneurship, leadership, and social innovation matches the kind of learning I crave: learning by doing, failing, reflecting, and building again. I’m especially impressed by how Nudge doesn’t treat students as passive listeners, but as co-creators who can shape communities.

From this fellowship, I want to take back three things. First, *clarity* — about how to convert a small idea into a project that actually helps people. Second, *courage* — to speak, lead, and take initiative even when I feel unsure. Third, *a network* of peers and mentors who challenge me to stay consistent after the program ends.

I won’t just take back certificates or memories. I want to take back a framework for action that I can use in my school, my panchayat, and later in my career. If one student from a small town like Koovappady can build something meaningful because of Nudge, then others will believe they can too. That ripple effect is why I want to be here.

I chose the Nudge Fellowship because it represents a rare opportunity to step outside my familiar environment and see my future through a completely new lens. Coming from Kerala, I have always felt a strong curiosity about the larger academic and professional spaces that exist beyond my state. The fellowship offers exactly that—a chance to immerse myself in Delhi’s dynamic ecosystem of learning, leadership, and youth-driven change, all in just one week and at zero cost.
What drew me the most is the promise of exposure. I want to experience first-hand how ideas are shaped, how leaders think, and how young people across India are building their paths. I believe that being in such an environment will help transform my curiosity into clarity. What I hope to take back with me is more than just information. I also hope to build meaningful connections with fellows, mentors, and educators who can continue to guide and inspire me beyond the program.
Most importantly, I expect to bring back a renewed belief in my own potential. A week in a new city, a new environment, and a new academic ecosystem can shape how I see myself and what I’m capable of becoming. That is why I chose the Nudge Fellowship.

name: Shiyan  Unneen mk

essay1:
സാധാരണക്കാരന്റെ ജീവിതത്തിൽ വലിയ മാറ്റങ്ങൾ കൊണ്ടുവരുന്ന ഒരു ഭരണസംവിധാനത്തിന്റെ ഭാഗമാകുക എന്നത് എന്റെ ജീവിതത്തിലെ ഏറ്റവും വലിയ സ്വപ്നമാണ്.  The Nudge ഇൻസ്റ്റിറ്റ്യൂട്ട് ഒരുക്കുന്ന ഈ ഫെല്ലോഷിപ്പ് എനിക്ക് വെറുമൊരു യാത്രയല്ല, മറിച്ച് എന്റെ ലക്ഷ്യങ്ങളിലേക്കുള്ള ആദ്യത്തെ ഉറച്ച ചുവടുവെപ്പാണ്.

ചെറുപ്പം മുതലേ നമ്മുടെ രാജ്യത്തെ നിയമങ്ങളെയും ചരിത്രത്തെയും കുറിച്ച് ആഴത്തിൽ അറിയാൻ എനിക്ക് വലിയ കൗതുകമായിരുന്നു. നമ്മുടെ ഭരണഘടന മുന്നോട്ടുവെക്കുന്ന നീതിയും തുല്യതയും ഓരോ സാധാരണക്കാരനിലേക്കും എങ്ങനെ എത്തുന്നു എന്ന് മനസ്സിലാക്കാൻ ഞാൻ ആഗ്രഹിക്കുന്നു. പുസ്തകങ്ങളിലൂടെ മാത്രം വായിച്ചറിഞ്ഞ ഡൽഹിയിലെ വലിയ സർവ്വകലാശാലകളുടെ മുറ്റത്ത് നിൽക്കാനും, അവിടുത്തെ അറിവിന്റെ ലോകം നേരിട്ട് അനുഭവിക്കാനും ഞാൻ അത്രമേൽ കൊതിക്കുന്നുണ്ട്. ഒരു സാധാരണ കുടുംബത്തിൽ നിന്നുള്ള ഒരാൾക്ക് ഇത്തരം വലിയ അവസരങ്ങൾ ലഭിക്കുക എന്നത് വലിയൊരു ഭാഗ്യമാണ്. അതുകൊണ്ട് തന്നെ, 100% സൗജന്യമായ ഈ ഫെല്ലോഷിപ്പ് എനിക്ക് നൽകുന്ന പ്രതീക്ഷയും ആത്മവിശ്വാസവും വാക്കുകൾക്ക് അപ്പുറമാണ്.

നമ്മുടെ രാജ്യം നേരിടുന്ന സാമൂഹിക പ്രശ്നങ്ങൾക്ക് യുക്തിസഹമായ പരിഹാരങ്ങൾ കണ്ടെത്താൻ എന്റെ കഴിവും ബുദ്ധിയും ഉപയോഗിക്കണമെന്ന് എനിക്ക് നിർബന്ധമുണ്ട്. പ്രത്യേകിച്ച്, സാധാരണക്കാർക്ക് ഭരണപരമായ കാര്യങ്ങൾ എളുപ്പത്തിൽ പ്രാപ്യമാക്കുന്ന ഒരു വ്യവസ്ഥിതി കെട്ടിപ്പടുക്കാൻ ഞാൻ ആഗ്രഹിക്കുന്നു. ഡൽഹിയിലെ മുതിർന്ന ഉദ്യോഗസ്ഥരിൽ നിന്നും മികച്ച അധ്യാപകരിൽ നിന്നും കാര്യങ്ങൾ പഠിക്കാൻ ലഭിക്കുന്ന ഓരോ നിമിഷവും ഞാൻ അങ്ങേയറ്റം വിലമതിക്കും.

ഈ ഏഴ് ദിവസത്തെ യാത്ര കഴിഞ്ഞ് മടങ്ങുമ്പോൾ, പുതിയൊരു വ്യക്തിയായി, എന്റെ നാടിന് വേണ്ടി എന്തെങ്കിലും ചെയ്യാനുള്ള കരുത്തുമായിട്ടായിരിക്കും ഞാൻ തിരികെ വരുന്നത്. എന്റെ കഴിവിൽ വിശ്വസിച്ച് എനിക്ക് ഈ അവസരം നൽകിയാൽ, അത് ഒരിക്കലും പാഴാക്കില്ലെന്ന് ഞാൻ ഉറപ്പുനൽകുന്നു. എന്റെ സ്വപ്നങ്ങൾക്കും ലക്ഷ്യങ്ങൾക്കും ചിറക് നൽകാൻ ഈ ഫെല്ലോഷിപ്പ് സഹായിക്കുമെന്ന് ഞാൻ ഉറച്ചു വിശ്വസിക്കുന്നു.
name: Munavvir Ali

essay1:
I am applying for The/Nudge Fellowship because I am deeply interested in understanding India through its people, cultures, and lived realities. Traveling across different states, observing regional cultures, and exploring their unique identities has always been a strong personal interest of mine. These experiences have helped me see diversity not just as a concept, but as something alive in everyday life.

Through such exposure, I have developed a growing curiosity about sociological perspectives—how communities live, interact, and evolve under different economic and cultural conditions. Each region presents its own set of realities, from economic challenges to social structures, and I find it meaningful to observe and learn from these differences. I believe that this fellowship will allow me to experience these aspects more closely and with greater purpose.

As a student from the Muslim community, I also carry a special interest in understanding the depth of Islamic traditions across India, particularly in culturally rich cities like Delhi. The presence of shrines, mosques, and historical legacies reflects a living heritage that continues to shape identities and communities. Exploring these spaces has given me a deeper sense of history, belonging, and continuity.

At the same time, I want to connect these cultural insights with broader social impact. I am interested in using creative mediums, such as media and storytelling, to document and share these realities in a way that informs and inspires others.

The/Nudge Fellowship offers me the opportunity to combine my curiosity, cultural interest, and desire to contribute meaningfully. I am eager to learn, engage, and grow through real-world experiences, and to better understand how I can play a role in creating a more inclusive and aware society.

Being exposed to India’s academic ecosystems in Delhi is not just about seeing something new—it is about seeing myself differently. I want to understand how people think, how opportunities are built, and how choices shape futures. I believe this experience will push me out of my comfort zone and help me grow in ways a classroom never could.
What I hope to take back is clarity—the kind that doesn’t come from advice, but from experience. I want to return with a stronger sense of who I am, what I am capable of, and where I truly want to go. Along with that, I hope to carry confidence, independence in my thinking, and the courage to take decisions that define my future.
For me, this fellowship is not just about exploring the world outside—it is about finally understanding the world within me.

Ibrahim  Khaleel
essay1:
My name is Ibrahim Khaleel, and I am a student from Narambadi in Kasaragod district, Kerala. I am currently pursuing my education with a focus on Islamic studies alongside my academic learning. I come from a region that has been deeply affected by the Endosulfan tragedy in Kasaragod, a man-made disaster that has caused severe health and developmental issues among many families.
Within my own family, my elder brother has been affected by this condition, with limited brain function and developmental challenges. Growing up alongside him, I have personally witnessed the struggles faced by individuals with disabilities—not only the medical difficulties but also the social discrimination and lack of equal treatment they often encounter. These experiences have shaped my perspective and created a strong desire in me to understand and address such issues.
However, due to my current academic path, I have had limited opportunities to engage in fieldwork or social initiatives. I see the Nudge Fellowship as a valuable opportunity to gain exposure and broaden my understanding.
Visiting Delhi would allow me to observe how a diverse and developed environment approaches inclusivity and supports individuals with disabilities. I hope to learn from these practices and explore how such approaches can be applied in my own community.
My long-term ambition is to become an IAS officer and contribute to building a more inclusive and equitable society. I believe this fellowship will be a meaningful step toward that goal.
I am eager to learn, observe, and grow through this opportunity.

essay1:
Nudge Fellowship എനിക്ക് എന്റെ present confusion കുറച്ച് clear ചെയ്യാൻ സഹായിക്കുന്ന ഒരു real opportunity പോലെ തോന്നുന്നു. ഞാൻ Plus One Humanities student ആണ്, honestly പറഞ്ഞാൽ എന്റെ future എന്താവണം എന്ന് ഇപ്പോഴും clear അല്ല. BA ചെയ്യണോ, law try ചെയ്യണോ, അല്ലെങ്കിൽ വേറെ എന്തെങ്കിലും explore ചെയ്യണോ എന്നതിൽ ഞാൻ ഇപ്പോഴും confused ആണ്. അതുകൊണ്ടാണ് ഈ fellowship എനിക്ക് important ആയി തോന്നുന്നത്.

Keralaയിൽ നിന്ന് Delhi വരെ പോയി അവിടെയുള്ള universities നേരിട്ട് കാണാൻ കഴിയുന്നത് എനിക്ക് വളരെ helpful ആയിരിക്കും. YouTube videos കണ്ടോ, മറ്റുള്ളവർ പറഞ്ഞോ അറിയുന്നതിനെക്കാൾ, നേരിട്ട് atmosphere feel ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ മാത്രമേ എനിക്ക് ശരിയായ idea കിട്ടൂ എന്ന് ഞാൻ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്നു. Different universities കാണുന്നതിലൂടെ എനിക്ക് ഏത് field ആണ് എനിക്ക് suit ആകുന്നത് എന്ന് better ആയി മനസ്സിലാക്കാം.

കൂടാതെ Delhiയിലെ important places കാണുന്നത് എന്റെ perspective widen ചെയ്യും എന്ന് ഞാൻ കരുതുന്നു.Delhi പോലെയൊരു വലിയcity എനിക്ക് ഒരു New experience തന്നെയാണ് , so ഇത് എനിക്ക് ഒരു completely new experience ആയിരിക്കും.

Personally, ഞാൻ daily lifeൽ വളരെ talkative അല്ല, പക്ഷേ camps പോലുള്ള ഇടങ്ങളിൽ ഞാൻ ആളുകളുമായി easily connect ആകുകയും, നല്ല company ആകുകയും ചെയ്യും. പുതിയ ആളുകളെ meet ചെയ്യാനും, പുതിയ environmentൽ adjust ചെയ്യാനും എനിക്ക് ഇഷ്ടമാണ്.

ഈ fellowship കഴിഞ്ഞാൽ, എനിക്ക് എന്റെ future കുറിച്ച് ഒരു clearer directionയും, കൂടുതൽ confidence ഉം കിട്ടണം എന്നതാണ് എന്റെ expectation.
